practical_description: Lightweight spacer layer designed to redirect impact energy laterally. Helps prevent trauma through deflection, not absorption. Prone to folding under tight stacking.
visual_description: Woven yellow-brown core with tight hex cells. Compresses with pressure and springs back. Feels like dry cardboard soaked in kevlar.

practical_description: Polyethylene foam insert with limited ballistic resistance. Dissipates energy, especially useful when layered behind strike plates. Melts or compresses on overmatch.
visual_description: White, soft-touch foam sheet with plasticky smell. Squeezable like dense packaging material. Scorches under friction.

practical_description: Flexible fabric infused with shear-thickening fluid. Soft under normal conditions, turns rigid when hit. Wears out under repeated strain.
visual_description: Slick textile, glossy under touch. Moves like rubber until struck, then feels like stone. Greasy gloss fades with age.

practical_description: Thin interstitial weave used to bond armor stacks. Deflects low-mass threats and spreads force between layers. Weak against core strikes.
visual_description: Ultra-thin woven sheet, golden with faint black threads. Moves like silk. Reflects light only at certain angles.

practical_description: Carbon nanotube mesh embedded in hardened resin. Designed to catch micro-shrapnel and spread energy. Cracks cleanly under overload.
visual_description: Dark grey panel with a diamond-grid shimmer. Rigid and unbending. Cold and smooth like a sculpted graphite slab.

practical_description: Hyperlight structure that collapses on impact, absorbing massive force. Best-in-class energy sink, but not durable. Often used as sacrificial core.
visual_description: Velvety matte black, floats in air when dropped. Feels like frozen smoke. Leaves graphite dust on fingers.

practical_description: Molybdenum disulfide-reinforced structure. Performs best against irregular fragments and shearing impacts. Crumbles at high temperatures.
visual_description: Slate grey composite with metallic sparkle. Feels like ceramic sponge. Emits dry crunch when flexed.

practical_description: Flexible and stab-resistant layer. Holds tension until punctured. Often used behind soft armor panels.
visual_description: Smooth translucent yellow foam, dense and elastic. Warms under pressure. Textured like sealed rubber honeycomb.
